name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 08.06.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 08.06.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 08.06.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 08.06.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on June 05 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 72.57 lakh ha (including 54.08 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 74.61 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on June 05 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 72.57 lakh ha (including 54.08 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 74.61 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in East India and many parts of Northeast India.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in East India and many parts of Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  June  01-  08  June  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 1%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 6%; (iii) Central India: 17%; and South Peninsula: 0%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  June  01-  08  June  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 1%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 6%; (iii) Central India: 17%; and South Peninsula: 0%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A fresh western Disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan Region from 11th June.
texts.text:  A fresh western Disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan Region from 11th June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall activity is likely in South India, many parts of Maharashtra, NE India, some parts of Central India, J&K, some parts of Punjab, some parts of Himachal Pradesh, and isolated places in Uttarakhand.
texts.text:  Rainfall activity is likely in South India, many parts of Maharashtra, NE India, some parts of Central India, J&K, some parts of Punjab, some parts of Himachal Pradesh, and isolated places in Uttarakhand.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Kerala,  some  parts  of  South  Interior  Karnataka, Arunachal  Pradesh,  while  it  is  below  normal  in  coastal  Karnataka,  Konkan-Goa,  Gujarat, Odisha, East India and the NMMT region.
texts.text:  Rainfall  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Kerala,  some  parts  of  South  Interior  Karnataka, Arunachal  Pradesh,  while  it  is  below  normal  in  coastal  Karnataka,  Konkan-Goa,  Gujarat, Odisha, East India and the NMMT region.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall (7-20cm) very likely over Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and northeast India during next 7 days with isolated extremely heavy rainfall also likely over Karnataka during 8th - 10th June.
texts.text:  Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall (7-20cm) very likely over Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and northeast India during next 7 days with isolated extremely heavy rainfall also likely over Karnataka during 8th - 10th June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures likely over Northwest India by 2-3°C till 10th June and Gradual fall by 2-4°C thereafter.  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over the rest parts of country till 13th June 2026.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures likely over Northwest India by 2-3°C till 10th June and Gradual fall by 2-4°C thereafter.  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over the rest parts of country till 13th June 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  04  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 54.726 billion cubic meter, which is 29.81% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 93.86 percent of last  year's  storage  and  118.58  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  04  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 54.726 billion cubic meter, which is 29.81% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 93.86 percent of last  year's  storage  and  118.58  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.
texts.text: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (28 May - 03 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 10%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 31 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 1%.
texts.text:  During the week (28 May - 03 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 10%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 31 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 1%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over the entire country except Konkan & Goa, Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, where it will be near normal to above normal during the week.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over the entire country except Konkan & Goa, Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, where it will be near normal to above normal during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (05 June  - 11 June 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (05 June  - 11 June 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual fall by 2-4°C in maximum temperatures likely  over many parts of Northwest India during 04 th -06 th June and gradual rise by 3-5°C during 07 th -09 th June.
texts.text:  Gradual fall by 2-4°C in maximum temperatures likely  over many parts of Northwest India during 04 th -06 th June and gradual rise by 3-5°C during 07 th -09 th June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant  change  in  maximum  temperatures  likely  over  East  India  till  10 th June  except  Bihar where rise in maximum temperature by 2-3°C till 06 th June.
texts.text:  No significant  change  in  maximum  temperatures  likely  over  East  India  till  10 th June  except  Bihar where rise in maximum temperature by 2-3°C till 06 th June.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (12 June  - 18 June 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (12 June  - 18 June 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures is  likely  to  be  above  normal NW India, East India, NE India, Central India and Gujarat.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures is  likely  to  be  above  normal NW India, East India, NE India, Central India and Gujarat.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low probability of Heatwave conditions likely over parts of northwest India (Uttar Pradesh) and parts of East India (Bihar, Jharkhand, Odisha, Gangetic West Bengal & Chhattisgarh) and adjoining central India (Madhya Pradesh) on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Low probability of Heatwave conditions likely over parts of northwest India (Uttar Pradesh) and parts of East India (Bihar, Jharkhand, Odisha, Gangetic West Bengal & Chhattisgarh) and adjoining central India (Madhya Pradesh) on some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Above-normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) to appreciably above-normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C)  maximum temperatures are likely in parts of East  India and the plains of northwest  India, and above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over parts of northeast India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during the week .
texts.text:  Above-normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) to appreciably above-normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C)  maximum temperatures are likely in parts of East  India and the plains of northwest  India, and above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over parts of northeast India. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during the week .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Northern Limit of Monsoon passes through 17.5°N/60°E, 17.5°N/65°E, 17°N/70°E, Devgad, Koppal,  Ananthapuramu,  Chennai,  15.5°N/85°E,  22°N/90.0°E,  Kailashahar,  Lamding,  North Lakhimpur and 29°N/95°E.
texts.text:  The Northern Limit of Monsoon passes through 17.5°N/60°E, 17.5°N/65°E, 17°N/70°E, Devgad, Koppal,  Ananthapuramu,  Chennai,  15.5°N/85°E,  22°N/90.0°E,  Kailashahar,  Lamding,  North Lakhimpur and 29°N/95°E.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of central Arabian Sea, Maharashtra, Karnataka, some parts of Telangana, some more parts of Andhra Pradesh,  remaining  parts  of  Tamil  Nadu  &  southwest  Bay  of  Bengal  and  some  more  parts  of westcentral & northwest Bay of Bengal, some parts of Chhattisgarh & Odisha, remaining parts of Northeastern  states  and  some  parts  of  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  &  Sikkim  during  next  3-4 days.
texts.text:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of central Arabian Sea, Maharashtra, Karnataka, some parts of Telangana, some more parts of Andhra Pradesh,  remaining  parts  of  Tamil  Nadu  &  southwest  Bay  of  Bengal  and  some  more  parts  of westcentral & northwest Bay of Bengal, some parts of Chhattisgarh & Odisha, remaining parts of Northeastern  states  and  some  parts  of  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  &  Sikkim  during  next  3-4 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. CROPS
texts.text: 2. CROPS

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Sugarcane: About 54.08 lakh ha area coverage under Sugarcane has been reported, which is the same as the area reported during the corresponding period of last year (54.08 lakh ha).
texts.text:  Sugarcane: About 54.08 lakh ha area coverage under Sugarcane has been reported, which is the same as the area reported during the corresponding period of last year (54.08 lakh ha).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cotton: About  7.51  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  9.72  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Cotton: About  7.51  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  9.72  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Jute :  About  6.18  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  6.08  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Jute :  About  6.18  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  6.08  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: * Other pulses (Other pulses- Kulthi & Mothbean), ** Average of 2022-23 & 2023-24)
texts.text: * Other pulses (Other pulses- Kulthi & Mothbean), ** Average of 2022-23 & 2023-24)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 04 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 54.726 BCM (29.81%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 04 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 54.726 BCM (29.81%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  125 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.
texts.text:  125 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  16 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.
texts.text:  16 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  25 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.
texts.text:  25 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION
texts.text: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 30 May - 05 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  Northern  and Central Karnataka, Punjab, Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh H.P., Tamil Nadu and J & K.
texts.text:  Soil moisture during 30 May - 05 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  Northern  and Central Karnataka, Punjab, Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh H.P., Tamil Nadu and J & K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil  moisture  is  better  than  the  average  of  the  past  10  years  in  parts  of  Northeastern  parts  of Bihar, Northern West Bengal and Ladakh.
texts.text:  Soil  moisture  is  better  than  the  average  of  the  past  10  years  in  parts  of  Northeastern  parts  of Bihar, Northern West Bengal and Ladakh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in pockets of Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Southern Karnataka, Western Maharashtra, Southern West Bengal, parts of Andhra Pradesh and in the Northeastern parts of Country
texts.text:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in pockets of Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Southern Karnataka, Western Maharashtra, Southern West Bengal, parts of Andhra Pradesh and in the Northeastern parts of Country
